sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: CIBC raises its price target on Endeavour Silver Corp. (EXK) to C$25 and keeps an outperformer rating
feed.summary: CIBC has increased its price target for Endeavour Silver Corp. (EXK) to C$25, maintaining an "outperformer" rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects CIBC's positive outlook on the company's financial prospects and market position.

feed.title: iShares MSCI Emerging Markets Asia ETF (NASDAQ: EEMA) - Share Price
feed.summary: This article provides detailed share price information for iShares MSCI Emerging Markets Asia ETF (NASDAQ: EEMA), including its current price of $98.41 and recent performance data. It outlines price movements over the current year, last year, and financial years, alongside a historical daily share price table. The data highlights EEMA's stock performance and volume over various periods.

feed.title: Biogen Targets Pediatric MS Growth With New Phase 3 Fumarate Study
feed.summary: Biogen (BIIB) has initiated a new Phase 3 clinical trial to evaluate the safety and efficacy of diroximel fumarate and dimethyl fumarate for treating relapsing forms of multiple sclerosis in pediatric patients. This study aims to provide a child-friendly oral treatment option and reinforce Biogen's long-term presence in the MS market, particularly as older products face generic competition. The trial, while not expected to impact short-term revenue, is a strategic move to potentially expand label indications and support the company's MS portfolio against rivals like Novartis's Gilenya.

feed.title: Chubb defeats $10 million property claim as court finds deliberate arson
feed.summary: Chubb successfully defended a $10 million property claim after a Queensland court concluded that a luxury estate fire was deliberately set, despite forensic evidence being inconclusive. The court based its decision on the property owner's dire financial situation, inconsistent accounts of the incident, and attempts to obstruct the investigation. The owner, Preston Richardson, who took his own life before the trial, was found to have intentionally caused the fire through circumstantial evidence.

feed.title: Mining shares led by Newmont plunge alongside precious metals as rate cut hopes fade (NEM:NYSE)
feed.summary: Mining company shares, including Newmont, are experiencing a significant decline, paralleling a plunge in precious metals. This downturn is attributed to rising inflation expectations and diminishing hopes for global interest rate cuts. Gold and silver themselves fell by 6% and 9% respectively, leading to investor fears.

feed.title: Cintas Corporation stock hits 52-week low at $180.33
feed.summary: Cintas Corporation's stock recently hit a new 52-week low of $180.39, marking a significant decline over the past year with a -4.75% total return, despite the company maintaining strong gross profit margins of 50.25%. This drop suggests investor caution or market pressures, though the company has a 34-year history of dividend payments and recently announced the acquisition of UniFirst Corporation for $5.5 billion. Analysts have reiterated varying ratings, with UBS and Truist Securities maintaining 'Buy' ratings, while Stifel holds a 'Hold' rating.

feed.title: North Carolina nonprofits can seek $25,000 to prepare for storms
feed.summary: Duke Energy and its Foundation are providing $500,000 in grants to strengthen storm preparedness across North Carolina, with individual grants of up to $25,000 available to eligible nonprofit and government organizations. This funding aims to support training, emergency planning, local response collaboration, and public education initiatives. Since 2016, the Foundation has invested $11.4 million in the state's preparedness and recovery efforts, demonstrating an ongoing commitment to community safety and resilience in the face of increasingly severe weather.

feed.title: Move Over Meta, Microsoft — Constellation, NextEra Hit Big Tech Valuations In 2026's Weirdest Trade
feed.summary: In a surprising market shift in 2026, utility companies Constellation Energy and NextEra Energy are achieving valuations comparable to major tech firms like Meta and Microsoft. This unusual rerating is largely driven by the explosive energy demand from AI data centers, positioning these electricity providers, especially those in clean energy, as new growth stocks. Meanwhile, traditional tech giants are undergoing a valuation recalibration due to high AI capital expenditures and slowing software growth.

feed.title: Arista (ANET) shares higher after Jim Cramer said it made sense in January 2025
feed.summary: Shares of Arista Networks (ANET) rose after Jim Cramer's comments regarding the stock in January 2025. This indicates a positive market reaction to his past endorsement of the company.

feed.title: National Legal and Policy Center Submits Shareholder Proposal to Dominion Energy
feed.summary: The National Legal and Policy Center has submitted a shareholder proposal to Dominion Energy, Inc. The proposal requests that Dominion Energy require two separate individuals to hold the offices of Chairman of the Board and Chief Executive Officer. This proposal is for the annual shareholder meeting scheduled for May 5, 2026.

feed.title: Corning Taps Meta AI Deal And Foldable Glass For Future Growth
feed.summary: Corning has secured a multiyear, $6 billion partnership with Meta Platforms to supply optical fiber and multicore solutions for AI data centers and introduced Gorilla Glass Ceramic 3 for foldable devices, with Motorola as an early adopter. These moves significantly broaden Corning's growth avenues into both AI infrastructure and consumer electronics. The company's stock has seen substantial gains, reflecting market excitement over these new product and customer developments.

feed.title: NRG Energy Tests AI PowerOS To Refine Portfolio And Earnings Story
feed.summary: NRG Energy has partnered with Equilibrium Energy to implement the AI-native PowerOS platform for optimizing its portfolio. This move aims to integrate advanced AI tools into NRG's operational decision-making, especially concerning its gas-heavy fleet and virtual power plant capacity. While this could enhance efficiency and potentially support earnings growth, it also introduces execution risks related to the integration of complex AI systems, especially given NRG's current financial metrics like interest coverage and profit margins.

feed.title: Apple fends off bid for new Apple Watch import ban at US trade tribunal
feed.summary: A U.S. trade tribunal has preliminarily ruled that current Apple Watches do not infringe patents owned by Masimo, rejecting Masimo's attempt to renew an import ban. Apple had previously altered its watches to circumvent a 2023 import ban, and a judge agreed that these redesigned watches do not violate Masimo's blood-oxygen reading technology patents. Although a separate appeals court affirmed the 2023 ban, Apple has resumed importing its redesigned watches and faces ongoing patent disputes with Masimo.

feed.title: Arvinas (ARVN): Citigroup Raises Price Target to $21.00 and Main
feed.summary: Citigroup analyst Yigal Nochomovitz has maintained a Buy rating on Arvinas (ARVN) and increased the price target from $18.00 to $21.00, reflecting a 16.67% upward revision. This latest change follows several other analyst rating adjustments for Arvinas in recent months. The average one-year price target from 17 analysts for ARVN is $15.24, implying a 41.07% upside from its current price of $10.80, with a consensus brokerage recommendation of "Outperform."

feed.title: Here’s How Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE)’s Shares Behaved After Jim Cramer Said “They’re A Big Winner” In January 2025
feed.summary: This article examines the stock performance of Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) since Jim Cramer endorsed it as a "big winner" in January 2025. HPE, a computer equipment company focusing on data centers, has seen its shares rise 36% over the past year and 3% since Cramer's comments. The article also notes a period of volatility in late 2025, where the stock initially dipped due to AI stock caution but then surged following its fourth-quarter earnings.

feed.title: Did Prologis’ (PLD) US$1.6 Billion GIC Venture Just Redefine Its Build-to-Suit Strategy?
feed.summary: Prologis and GIC have formed a US$1.6 billion joint venture to develop and own build-to-suit logistics facilities across major U.S. markets. This partnership strengthens Prologis's focus on customized warehouses but comes amidst an overhang from a significant legal case with potential damages exceeding US$1 billion. The article suggests this venture reinforces Prologis’s long-term strategy for modern logistics spaces, though investors should monitor both growth initiatives and legal risks.

feed.title: SAP Recognized as a Six-Time Leader in the 2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Integration Platform as a Service
feed.summary: SAP has been recognized as a Leader for the sixth consecutive time in the 2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Integration Platform as a Service (iPaaS). This acknowledgment is attributed to SAP's continuous investment in a modern, scalable, and AI-ready integration platform, SAP Integration Suite, which caters to complex hybrid and multicloud environments. The suite, part of SAP Business Technology Platform, offers API management, event-driven architecture, process integration, and AI-assisted development, enabling customers to connect systems, orchestrate workflows, and comply with regulations globally.

feed.title: DYLG Stock Fund Price and Chart — AMEX:DYLG
feed.summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the Global X Dow 30 Covered Call & Growth ETF (DYLG), traded on the NYSE American. It includes key financial metrics like Assets Under Management (AUM), dividend yield, and expense ratio, along with details about its passive management style and the Cboe DJIA Half BuyWrite Index it tracks. The fund aims to offer growth potential and yield from the Dow Jones Industrial Average by using a covered call strategy on half of its portfolio.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges Eos Energy
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC has announced a class action lawsuit against Eos Energy Enterprises, Inc. (NASDAQ: EOSE) and its officers. The lawsuit, filed on behalf of investors who purchased Eos Energy securities between November 5, 2025, and February 26, 2026, alleges that the company made materially false and misleading statements and failed to disclose critical operational issues. Investors are urged to join the case by May 5, 2026, to recover damages for alleged violations of federal securities laws.

feed.title: AC Immune (ACIU) CEO discloses equity and option holdings
feed.summary: AC Immune SA's CEO, Andrea Pfeifer, has filed an initial statement of beneficial ownership, revealing her holdings in the company. She directly owns 3,840,288 Common Shares, which includes 1,921,005 Common Shares from restricted share units, and indirectly holds 14,000 Common Shares via her spouse. The filing also details fully vested share options with exercise prices between $2.03 and $9.53, expiring from 2027 to 2032.

feed.title: Best-In-Class Claims Grow For Kelun/Merck’s Sac-TMT In Lung Cancer
feed.summary: New data regarding Kelun/Merck's Sac-TMT in lung cancer will be presented at the upcoming European Lung Cancer Congress in Copenhagen. This presentation follows Kelun's recent world-first TROP2 ADC approval in lung cancer. The article highlights growing "best-in-class" claims for the drug.

feed.title: Law Offices of Frank R. Cruz Encourages Gartner, Inc. (IT) Shareholders To Inquire About Securities Fraud Class Action
authors: Law Offices of Frank R. Cruz
feed.summary: The Law Offices of Frank R. Cruz has filed a class action lawsuit against Gartner, Inc. (IT) on behalf of shareholders who purchased stock between February 4, 2025, and February 2, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Gartner made misleading statements and failed to disclose issues regarding its contract value growth rate, leading to significant stock price drops on August 5, 2025, and February 3, 2026. Shareholders have until May 18, 2026, to file a lead plaintiff motion.

feed.title: Lance Martin Leaves Coldwell Banker to Join eXp Realty
feed.summary: Veteran Southern California broker Lance Martin, known for his nearly 40-year real estate career, has moved his 140-agent organization, The Martin Group / Cobalt Realty Group, from Coldwell Banker to eXp Realty after 24 years. Martin stated this decision reflects his belief that the traditional legacy franchise model is broken and that eXp Realty's agent-centric approach positions agents more effectively. He plans to leverage eXp's model to expand his organization across multiple states.

feed.title: Your Apple TV Is Completely Changing How It Recommends Movies And Shows
feed.summary: Apple is set to launch tvOS 26.4, introducing a new "Genius Browse" feature to the Apple TV app that will redefine movie and show recommendations. This update coincides with the phasing out of iTunes Movies and iTunes TV Show apps, aiming to offer users a more intuitive content discovery experience. The Genius Browse will provide consistently updated recommendations from various services, and its introduction might also lay the groundwork for a more personalized Apple TV experience, potentially powered by an enhanced Siri.

feed.title: Jeep and Dodge EV drivers can now use Tesla chargers with an adapter
feed.summary: Stellantis announced that owners of electric vehicles from its brands like Dodge, Jeep, Ram, Fiat, and Maserati can now access Tesla's Supercharger network in North America using an adapter. This expansion adds over 27,500 Tesla V3 and V4 Supercharger stalls, aligning Stellantis with more than 20 other EV manufacturers that have already integrated with Tesla's charging infrastructure. Drivers will need a Stellantis-brand NACS adapter, though some Tesla stations feature a built-in "Magic Dock" adapter, and the 2027 Dodge Charger Daytona will natively support NACS.

feed.title: Gold Tumbles Over 6%; Accenture Earnings Top Views
feed.summary: U.S. stocks traded lower midway through Thursday, with the Nasdaq Composite, Dow, and S&P 500 all declining. Accenture (NYSE: ACN) reported stronger-than-expected fiscal second-quarter earnings but issued a cautious outlook. Commodities saw significant movement, with gold falling over 6% and silver down over 10%, while oil traded higher.

feed.title: ConocoPhillips to post Q1 results before market open April 30
feed.summary: ConocoPhillips (NYSE: COP) will host a conference call webcast on Thursday, April 30, 2026, at 12:00 p.m. Eastern time to discuss its first-quarter 2026 financial and operating results. The company's financial and operating results will be released before the market opens on the same day. Interested parties can register for the webcast on ConocoPhillips' Investor Relations site.

feed.title: ConocoPhillips to hold first-quarter earnings conference call on Thursday, April 30
feed.summary: ConocoPhillips (NYSE: COP) will host a conference call webcast on Thursday, April 30, 2026, at 12:00 p.m. Eastern time to discuss its first-quarter 2026 financial and operating results. The financial results will be released before the market opens on the same day. Interested parties can access the webcast and register through ConocoPhillips' Investor Relations site.

feed.title: ConocoPhillips to hold first-quarter earnings conference call on Thursday, April 30
feed.summary: ConocoPhillips (NYSE: COP) will host a conference call webcast on Thursday, April 30, 2026, at 12:00 p.m. Eastern time to discuss its first-quarter 2026 financial and operating results. The company's financial and operating results will be released before the market opens on April 30. Interested parties can access the webcast by registering on ConocoPhillips' Investor Relations site.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges Gartner, Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC has announced a class action lawsuit against Gartner, Inc. (NYSE: IT), alleging that the company and its officers made materially false and misleading statements to investors between February 4, 2025, and February 2, 2026. The lawsuit claims that Gartner overstated its expected contract value growth, projected revenues, and confidence in non-federal CV rates, while also misrepresenting its consulting segment's performance. Investors who suffered losses are encouraged to join the lawsuit by May 18, 2026.

feed.title: General Mills CEO puts 'difficult financial results behind' the company
feed.summary: General Mills CEO Jeffrey Harmening announced that the company has moved past a period of difficult financial results, despite a significant drop in net earnings for the fiscal 2026 third quarter due to restructuring, reinvestment, and divestiture costs. The company reaffirmed its full-year guidance, expecting improved performance in Q4 due to timing comparisons, the 53rd week, and market share momentum. Harmening expressed confidence in delivering improved organic sales growth in fiscal 2027 by focusing on brand remarkability and cost efficiency.

feed.title: AI to Resolve 60% of Supply Chain Disruptions by 2031: Gartner
feed.summary: Gartner predicts that by 2031, artificial intelligence will resolve 60% of supply chain disruptions without human intervention, leading to increasingly autonomous supply chains. Chief Supply Chain Officers (CSCOs) are embracing agentic AI capabilities and will need to implement new governance models to oversee AI-enabled decision-making responsibly. While current technological immaturity limits full automation to low-risk decisions, AI advancements are expected to be the most influential driver of future supply chain performance.

feed.title: Dow's 365-point fall led by losses in shares of Boeing, Sherwin-Williams Co.
feed.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average experienced a 365-point decline, primarily driven by significant losses in the stock values of Boeing and Sherwin-Williams Co. This drop indicates a negative close for the Dow.

feed.title: Phillips 66 stock hits all-time high at 178.5 USD
feed.summary: Phillips 66 (PSX) stock has reached an all-time high of $178.50, reflecting a 38.82% increase over the past year and a market capitalization of $71.16 billion. The company's strong performance is attributed to strategic initiatives and favorable market conditions, with its stock identified as undervalued by InvestingPro. Recent news includes a Q4 2025 EPS beat, a $2.25 billion credit agreement, new board appointments, and a raised price target from TD Cowen.

feed.title: Confluent: Reusable Data Streams Are Rising C-Level IT Agendas
feed.summary: Confluent has launched Confluent Cloud for Apache Flink, a fully managed service designed to enable organizations to process data in real-time and create reusable data streams. This offering aims to streamline business operations for various technology use cases like fraud detection and real-time inventory management. The company emphasizes the role of stream processing in providing immediate insights and contributing to cleaner, greener data practices.

feed.title: News - MetLife Japan probes suspected unauthorized data transfer affecting thousands of records
feed.summary: MetLife Japan is investigating a suspected unauthorized data transfer by an employee that could affect thousands of records, potentially making it one of the largest incidents of its kind in Japan's life insurance sector. The U.S.-based life insurer's Japanese unit, MetLife Insurance K.K., is looking into the incident. The article places this event in the context of recent data breaches affecting other companies.

feed.title: Tesla's robotaxi crash count reaches 15 since June in Austin. Waymo hits 60.
feed.summary: Tesla and Waymo have reported additional crashes involving their robotaxi fleets in Austin, facing increased scrutiny over their self-driving technology. Tesla, with fewer than 40 cars, now has 15 reported crashes since June, while Waymo, operating 200 fully driverless vehicles, has logged 60 crashes in the same period. These reports come as Tesla prepares for the production launch of its purpose-built Cybercab robotaxi and both companies face ongoing investigations into their safety records.

feed.title: Horizon Kinetics (TPL) adds to Texas Pacific Land stake with open-market buy
feed.summary: Horizon Kinetics Asset Management LLC, a more than 10% owner of Texas Pacific Land Corp (TPL), reported an open-market purchase of 1 share of TPL common stock at $531.96 per share. Following this transaction, Horizon Kinetics directly holds 3,467,928 shares of TPL common stock. The filing also notes that Murray Stahl does not exercise investment discretion over these securities.

feed.title: Blackstone Raises $12 Billion for Asia PE Fund as It Nears Close
feed.summary: Blackstone Inc. has raised over $12 billion for its newest Asia-Pacific buyout fund, Blackstone Capital Partners Asia III, exceeding its target. The fund focuses on investments in India, Japan, and Australia to enhance private equity returns and is expected to close in the coming weeks.

feed.title: Amex soups up cards for Concur
feed.summary: American Express is integrating virtual cards into SAP's Concur expense service, allowing companies to issue virtual cards with spending limits and enhanced security for business expenses. This integration, following previous real-time expense notifications, aims to automate and streamline tedious expense processes for employees and administrators. The new feature is currently available to select U.S.-based American Express corporate and business customers but will expand access in the third quarter.

feed.title: Purpose Enbridge (ENB) Yield Shares ETF Declares Monthly Dividen
feed.summary: Purpose Enbridge (ENB) Yield Shares ETF has declared a monthly dividend of CAD 0.11 per share, payable on April 2 to shareholders of record as of March 27. While Enbridge Inc. shows strong revenue growth and profitability, its financial health analysis indicates high debt levels with a low Altman Z-Score, suggesting potential distress. The company's valuation metrics are near historical highs, and analyst sentiment is mixed with limited upside potential.

feed.title: Transdigm Group stock hits 52-week low at 1182.73 USD
feed.summary: Transdigm Group (TDG) stock has fallen to a new 52-week low of $1,182.73, reflecting a 6.5% decline over the past year and a 9.8% drop year-to-date. This downturn occurs despite strong gross profit margins and revenue growth, amid broader market uncertainties and industry pressures. The company recently announced $2 billion in new debt to finance acquisitions, while analysts have offered mixed ratings, with some downgrading due to concerns over slowing aftermarket growth and others raising price targets based on strong margins.

feed.title: Apple Stock (AAPL): Price Target, Forecast & Analysis 2026
feed.summary: This comprehensive analysis of Apple (AAPL) stock in March 2026 examines its premium valuation, record-breaking Q1 FY2026 earnings driven by the iPhone 17 super-cycle and a resurgence in China, and its capital-light AI strategy leveraging a landmark partnership with Google's Gemini. The article details Apple's $109 billion Services growth engine, past stock performance, analyst price targets, and risks such as geopolitical tensions, valuation premium, and regulatory headwinds. It concludes that while not a bargain, AAPL remains a high-quality equity with compelling setups for the next 12-18 months, recommending dollar-cost averaging for long-term investors.

feed.title: Preferred holders get April 15 payout from Entergy Texas
feed.summary: Entergy Texas (ETI) announced a quarterly dividend of $0.3359375 per share for its Series A Preferred Stock. The dividend is scheduled to be paid on April 15, 2026, to shareholders of record as of April 3, 2026. This declaration specifically pertains to Series A preferred shares and does not affect common stock dividends.

feed.title: Fat Brands CEO agrees to temporary leave of absence
feed.summary: Fat Brands CEO Andrew Wiederhorn has agreed to a temporary leave of absence during the company's Chapter 11 bankruptcy process to secure debtor-in-possession financing. This decision comes after creditors pushed for his removal due to a stock sale without court approval, leading to an agreement where Wiederhorn's family members will leave, and the board will resign, with a special committee overseeing operations. The financing is crucial for Fat Brands to maintain operations and prepare for a sale process starting in April.

feed.title: Buying pressure lifts Energy Transfer stock higher in today's trading
feed.summary: Energy Transfer LP (ET) stock is up 2.06% today, trading at $19.05, and is positioned above all major moving averages, signaling a positive trend. Analysts have upgraded price targets post-earnings, highlighting the company's undervaluation and strong fundamentals, supporting bullish sentiment despite some mixed oscillator signals. The $19.50 resistance level is identified as a key point to monitor for a potential near-term breakout.

feed.title: Prologis forms $1.6B JV to develop logistics facilities
feed.summary: Prologis has announced a $1.6 billion joint venture with GIC to develop 4.1 million square feet of build-to-suit logistics space across major U.S. markets. This partnership aims to leverage Prologis' development platform and GIC's long-term investment strategy, focusing on pre-leased, custom-designed facilities. The initiative underscores a growing trend in companies investing in resilient distribution networks, with build-to-suit projects making up a significant portion of Prologis' recent development starts.

feed.title: LS Power strikes deal to buy 4.4 GW in PJM from Constellation
feed.summary: LS Power has agreed to acquire five gas-fired power plants totaling 4.4 GW in the PJM Interconnection from Constellation Energy for approximately $5 billion. This transaction was necessitated by conditions set by the U.S. Department of Justice and the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission following Constellation's acquisition of Calpine, aimed at preventing market power issues. The deal, which involves plants in Delaware and Pennsylvania, is expected to close late this year pending regulatory approvals.

feed.title: Is Amcor Stock Outperforming the Nasdaq?
feed.summary: Amcor Plc (AMCR) is a large-cap packaging company with a global footprint. While AMCR has marginally outperformed the Nasdaq Composite ($NASX) over the past three months, it has lagged the index significantly year-to-date and over the past 52 weeks. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for AMCR, with a price target suggesting a 35.2% upside.

feed.title: The Zacks Analyst Blog Highlights Exxon Mobil, Cheniere Energy, Franco-Nevada, Lockheed Martin and RTX
feed.summary: Amid global geopolitical tensions, rising oil prices, and high inflation, three sectors are poised to benefit: energy, precious metals & mining, and defense & strategic infrastructure. Exxon Mobil and Cheniere Energy are noted in the energy sector, Franco-Nevada in precious metals, and Lockheed Martin and RTX Corp. in defense, as these industries offer potential hedges and growth opportunities in a complex market environment.
